column1: # miniOS CNC operator UI -- 1080x1920 portrait canvas.
#   x and y on every widget are ABSOLUTE within the 1080x1920 page,
#   even when parent= is set. The parent= field only governs grouping
#   for show/hide and event routing -- it does NOT translate the
#   widget's coordinates. A label "inside" a panel at y=120 needs its
#   own absolute y (e.g. y=190 to sit 70 px below the panel top).
#   page bg          #0A0F1A   one tone, very dark slate
#   inset bg         #0F172A   nested cards / readout boxes
#   text accent      #7DD3FC   axis letters, key data
#   neutral button   #334155   PREV / NEXT / CLEAR / BACK
#   DRO border lin   #1E40AF   X / Y / Z digit border
#   DRO border rot   #92400E   A digit border
#   wcs accent       #7C3AED   purple for active WCS
# TYPOGRAPHY (kernel scale= integer maps to 8x16 base glyph)
#   xs body / units     scale=1   (8x16) -- captions, units, inline labels
#   md section title    scale=2   (16x32) -- panel headers, section labels
#   lg page title       scale=2   (16x32) -- centered page title at top
#   lg button label     scale=2   (16x32) -- primary action buttons
#   xl DRO digits       scale=3   (24x48) -- DRO commanded position
#   The kernel Panel renders title= text at y+10, sitting on the top
#   border. With our left margins it gets clipped. So we DROP panel
#   title= entirely and use a dedicated label widget for the section
#   header, placed at panel-top + 14 px (so a panel at y=120 has its
#   header label at y=134, scale=2). The first 50 px of every content
#   panel is reserved for the header strip.
#   bottom_nav owns y=1740..1880. Every page sets its root panel to
#   h=1740 so it does not collide. The nav has 7 evenly-spaced buttons
#   in one cool slate palette; the active page is highlighted via the
#   button's brighter background only when the page id matches.
# bottom_nav -- shared 7-button bar (included by every page).
# 7 tiles at 140 px with 10 px gaps fill 1040 px of the 1080-wide canvas.
#   Dashboard . Jog . MDI . Program . View . Offsets . Service
# Macros is reachable from the dashboard (operator-critical only here).
# Buttons share the same neutral slate; their accent comes from the
# small color stripe via the border. active_if=page:N does not work
# because page binds to an integer page index, so we keep all buttons
# uniform and let the user rely on the page title for orientation.
#   y  110..1030  (panel y=110, h=920) DRO + cycle row (split L/R)
#   y 1520..1700  (panel y=1520, h=200) macros + alarms quick row
# Mode selector — operator's declared intent. The active mode is highlighted
# via active_if=operator_mode:N. Display-only today; cycle-start gating is a
# X axis row -- letter at y=176, digit field y=170, sub-row y=240
# Feed + spindle + load (inside DRO panel, y=680..1010)
# --- macros / alarms quick row (panel y=1520, h=200) ---
# jog -- manual axis jog with selectable axis and increment.
# JOG-/JOG+ are discrete step (one click = jog_increment counts).
# HOLD-/HOLD+ are press-to-toggle continuous: first tap starts axis
# motion at jog_feed_cps, second tap on either button stops it.
# Strict hold-to-move would need a TouchDown/TouchUp BuilderEvent
# pair; the current Button widget only fires on TouchUp. Toggle
# semantics ship today; re-evaluate when the renderer grows hold.
# Load + mode + WCS + alarm strip (compacted to free room for spindle).
# Spindle strip — RPM input + START/STOP/REV. RPM commits via the
# generic input pipeline (commit:spindle:rpm); REV negates the current
# requested rpm and re-starts. START/STOP route to spindle:start/stop.
# Hold-/Hold+ resolve the axis at click time from selected_axis: each
# action target carries its sign and the dispatcher reads the currently
# selected axis. Format jog:hold:<axis>:<sign>; renderer-side state is
# per-axis so toggling on a different axis selector keeps the previous
# axis moving until its own button is tapped again.

column8: text=Type a G-code line then press Enter or SUBMIT. History is in-RAM only.

column1: # Renders the authored kinematic chain through render_live_machine.
# Toggle buttons flip operator_api view flags consulted each frame.
# Overlay camera controls in viewport bottom-right (absolute coords)
#   y   90.. 950 two-column row: file list (left, w=540) + preview (right, w=480)
#   y  970..1090 transport buttons (PREV / NEXT / REBUILD / CYCLE)
#   y 1110..1310 restart strip (GOTO LINE + AT BLOCK + BLOCKS)
# Each list row uses a button + 3 labels: the button highlights via
# active_if=program:N:loaded:1 so the operator sees which file is bound to
# channel 0; the labels surface name and human-readable size from the
# FAT32 mount. Tapping a row fires program:select:N (load + open).
# Row pitch = 90 px starting at y=170 -> 8 rows occupy y=170..890

column8: text=type N, press Enter to restart

column1: # offsets -- WCS table + tool table + active editor.
#   y  190..330  active editor strip (X Y Z A inputs)
# Per-slot live readouts (G54..G59). Buttons sit at y=460,530,600,670,740,810
# so the value rows ride 18 px lower (y+18) to align with the button text.
# Per-slot live readouts (T1..T8). Buttons sit on a 75 px row pitch from
# y=1040; values ride 22 px lower (y+22) to centre on the button text.
# Edit strip: keep the active-tool inputs at the bottom of the panel
# (was inside the table; relocated here so the per-slot rows stay clean).
# Two rows: ETHERCAT / ALARMS / MACROS on top, HOMING wizard solo on the
# second so it has full-width visibility — operators land on this page when
# something is wrong, the homing wizard is part of recovery.

column8: text=- Setup snapshots persist offsets, tool table and active modes.
text=- ETHERCAT shows live bus health (period, p99, max, fault).
text=- ALARMS routes to recovery + acknowledgement workflow.
text=- PROBE CYCLES launches the qualified macro library.

column1: # ethercat -- bus diagnostics page (master state, cycle health, fault)

column8: text=Master target cycle: 250 us. Miss = late delivery; trips = deadline-fault latches.

column1: #   y   90.. 270 axis selector (4 buttons + per-axis homed indicators)
#   y  290.. 690 method library (6 strategies, 2 rows of 3)
#   y  710.. 880 speeds panel (fast / slow + accel notes)
#   y  900..1180 status panel (state hero + message + per-axis homed strip)
# Operator path: pick axis -> pick strategy -> tap HOME. The HOME button
# routes to homing:start which calls ethercat::Master::run_homing_sequence
# under the deadline-fault gate. ABORT zeroes axis velocity and resets the
# Per-axis homed strip (mirrors the dashboard convention)
# alarms -- active alarm list, per-row acknowledge, history clear
#   y   90..1200  active alarms panel (h=210 summary + h=900 table)

column8: text=Showing first 4 active alarms. ACK clears the row from active state.
text=- PREV / NEXT walks the loaded macro library.
text=- RUN executes the selected macro on its target channel.
text=- ABORT stops the active macro and restores READY.
text=- Probe macros are surfaced on the PROBE page.

column1: #   y   90.. 470 wizard panel: 6 cycle buttons (active = picked)
#   y  490.. 760 status panel: state hero + cycle name + step/total + msg
#                + REFERENCE SPHERE button (only cycle backed by probe::Runtime)
#   y  780.. 1000 result panel: PX/PY/PZ capture + VALID flag
#   y 1020..1240 controls: START / ABORT / ACCEPT / REJECT
# Operator path: pick cycle -> Confirming -> tap START -> Running ->
# on completion the wizard sits in Inspecting until ACCEPT or REJECT.
# ABORT stops the active backend at any time. START is a no-op while
# either EtherCAT master holds a deadline-fault latch. Sphere uses the
# probe::Runtime backend; the other six cycles execute as macros.
# Each control button highlights via active_if on the wizard state in
# which it is enabled (Confirming=2 for START, Running=3 for ABORT,
# Row 1 (y=150): Qualify / Z Surface / X Edge
# Row 2 (y=300): Y Edge / Bore Center / 3D Pocket
# Sphere selector sits in the status header — wider single tap because
# reference-sphere is the only cycle backed by probe::Runtime directly.
